hard.next_step: Mar 27, 2026: talk with Adtian Discussion Summary: • You confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO’s broader SaaS and internal build strategy. • You reiterated that this is not a reflection on Zycus, and that you still see strong value in our domain expertise. • You outlined a desire to pivot toward a design-focused engagement to map current processes, maturity, gaps, and AI readiness before reconsidering a solution. Next Steps: • Adrian to share a written outline of the requirements and expectations for the design/scoping workshop and include Head of Sourcing and Head of Legal. • We will review your outline and come back with a proposed approach, scope, and commercials for a design engagement. • Once aligned, we will schedule a scoping workshop to assess current processes, identify gaps, and discuss AI use cases and readiness. We appreciate your transparency and would really like to support you in defining the best path forward. Mar 25, 26: Got a private message from Adrian that there was a structure change and this causes some extra clarification.Legal has proposed the following but still no feedback from Adrian: To ensure we lock this deal within March, we can proceed with a backdated approach by keeping the MSA execution/effective date on or before March 31. At the same time, we can specify that the SaaS subscription period will commence in the first week of April. This ensures that the contract can be executed in that timeframe, while also protecting the customer from losing any days in the SaaS term, as the subscription will effectively begin upon actual signing (i.e., around the first week of April). lets see if this approach works out. Mar 23, 2026: Just received this acknowledgment from Adrian: Thank you, Caroline. I’m consulting internally and will provide feedback by EOD.Kind Regards, Adrian Mar 23, 2026: They proposed to reduce the contract term - we went with a counter proposal of 4 years plus increase as they lose the bundle discount. We also proposed a 3+2. Waiting for feedback. Mar 17, 202: Adrain responeded to Johns request for an update after the AI counceli call last week The internal discussion initially focused on AI considerations but expanded to the overall contracting approach. Proposed change in implementation sequencing: Phase 1: Sourcing and Global Design Phase 2: Contract Management (originally Phase 1) Purpose: address more immediate business needs. Proposal for a shorter initial contract term of 3 years instead of 5, aiming for a more streamlined commercial setup. No final decision yet; a follow-up discussion is scheduled later this week. Request for feedback on potential impacts of the phase adjustment (delivery, resourcing, commercial, timeline). Interim update provided considering your financial year-end; further clarity will follow after the next internal meeting. Caroline reached out to Adrian via WhatsApp and email for a call today Mar 02, 2026: Met with Adrian, still one extra Board to go through - CTO, AI vetting Board. He wants the pricing to go back to the original 500 contratcs with tiering to 1000 more in year 2. Adrian also wants to know if anything is new on Merlin. John confirmed we can do 6% uplift instead of 7%. Adrian think we can get signatures around week March 16th if the AI vetting board goes smooth. Feb 20, 2026: contracting ongoing Feb 16, 2026: Caroline suggested onsite meeting 26/2 when we are in Cape Town Next steps: Integration meeting with Werner and Nishita Confirm SOW meeting with Hrishi and Adrian Confirm with Zoheb Order Form 1 February 13, 2026: Contract discussion ongoing with legal and SOW being drafted. Jan 20, 2026: Caroline sent an email to Adrian with proposed date for legal call but got an OOO sick leave notice from Adrian. Jan 19, 2026: REceived redlined agreements from Capitec and shared with Zoheb - email from Adrian - Please find attached agreements with our comments, there’s a couple of items outstanding on our side, but we can clarify this as we go. I will work with you to find a slot that works for us both to discuss the contract. Jan 08, 26: They are holding a contract alignment session on Friday next week to pick up the contracting work again and close out the outstanding items. the outcomes and proposed next steps will be shared immediately thereafter, including a few options for a joint call with Zycus Legal. Dec 12, 2025: We have received an LOI which states we are the chosen vendor, email from project lead: I will try and summarise our standard approval flow below: Internal Contract Review Supplier and Capitec Contract Review (multiple rounds) IT Review (which I believe has been completed as we did this in parallel) Following 2 above we move into approval according to Delegation of Authority Contract Signature. We’re currently in stage 2, having reviewed your comments and sharing it with the relevant stakeholders, this has resulted in more consultation needed - we need to resolve this before issuing you with the draft contract for further review. I will set up a call with our respective Legal dept when we concluded internal consultation. We will pick this up as soon as everyone is back from the December break. Dec 05, 2025 E mail sent to Adrian offering a further 2 months FOC extension year 1 with signature on or before 31/12. No response as yet. Dec 01, 2025 Confidence level 90% Dec 01, 2025 Questions on terms for terms payment Nov 27, 2025 – Capitec Bank Update Adrian sent an email requesting if they can pay the SaaS fees as per milestone implementation. This request was declined by Zycus upper management Menachem drafted and sent an email to Adrian explaining the reasons behind the SaaS upfront payment Next steps: Capitec Exco team is meeting on Monday Dec 1. to finalize the project signing off.
hard.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma notes from a BPMN process-redesign workshop capture Capitec asking Zycus to 'outline the process design methodology and include design principles (e.g., start and end points, AI/bot considerations) in the proposal.' AI is mentioned as one of multiple design principles, not as a primary buying driver. Deal scope is core S2C (eProcurement, iContract) without Merlin, ANA, or Merlin Intake ‚Äî AI is not a product-scope headline.
• Formal Evaluation with very high meeting cadence (17 Avoma meetings) and engaged stakeholders including Adrian Jacobs, Johan Kotze, Isabella Rankine, Lynn Dudley-Owen plus the Letsema consulting partner. The project has pivoted to a technology-agnostic process design phase with a June 2026 start ‚Äî this explicitly delays direct tool/AI selection in favour of process redesign first. BD_Engagement listed as NaN.
• Medium confidence: meeting evidence is rich (17 calls) but the buyer has explicitly de-prioritised tool selection (including AI capability) in favour of process redesign. Default DOWN at 5/6 boundary held; would move UP only if Capitec returns to evaluating Merlin/agentic capabilities in a Phase 2 tool-selection sprint after process design completes.

north_star_verdict.headline: The design-engagement contract is stalled on Adrian Jacobs, who is sitting on the SOW and MSA past the late-June signature target while Lynn Dudley-Owen drives kickoff and workshop scheduling; the original 218k solution remains paused since March behind Capitec's CIO-sponsored internal-build strategy.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-12-31 is 178 days out but reflects the paused 218k solution, not the live scope. Solution paused Mar 2026; the live deal is a 10-week technology-agnostic design engagement (kickoff July 7, workshops July 21-Aug 11, blueprint by ~week 10). In Formal Evaluation since created May 2024 (over 790 days) because the stage label still tracks the paused solution. Last two-way buyer touch 2026-06-12 (24 days ago). Design-engagement signature was targeted for late June and is now overdue with the SOW un-returned by Adrian; a key action is stalled on him, so this reads Slowing.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (or Omit): the 218k solution is paused; only the smaller technology-agnostic design engagement is live, so the current amount and category overstate committed value.
evidence: Avoma S2C Review Workshop Schedule 2026-06-12: Hrishikesh: 'both SOW and MSA are with Adrian... hasn't come back with any red lines... what concerns me is SOW, I don't have visibility. Adrian hasn't updated me on that.'
Avoma 15 Min touchbase 2026-06-01: Hrishikesh: 'thinking we'll all close the contracts by then' (mid-June kickoff placeholder), signature since slipped.

SF Next_Step__c 2026-03-27: 'confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO's broader SaaS and internal build strategy... pivot toward a design-focused engagement.'
Avoma S2C Contract/Scoping discussion 2026-04-28: Adrian Jacobs: 'the solution is out of scope for now, hence the brief mentions that the design would be technology agnostic.'
SF Next_Step__c 2025-12-12: LOI received naming Zycus the chosen vendor; standard approval flow (internal review, supplier + Capitec review, IT review, Delegation of Authority, signature).
SF field history 2026-06-26: CloseDate 2026-09-30 -> 2026-12-31 (owner-adjusted); SF LastActivityDate 2026-06-12.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists on any call or Salesforce field. Capitec articulated qualitative pain only: on the 2026-04-28 scoping call Kamen (Letsema) sized procurement at roughly 18 first-line staff plus about 30 more being centralized on PO creation, and said processes had 'gone through the most changes we have in the last 5 years' with 'documentation probably outdated' and the end-to-end map 'outdated already.' No cycle-time, cost-per-transaction, FTE-hour, error-rate, or savings figure has ever been stated. The 10-week design engagement is itself scoped to surface current-state metrics, so the move is to use its discovery workshops to quantify baseline pain and build an ROI/TCO case against the internal-build alternative.

economic_buyer.narrative: The true budget authority is Capitec's CIO, who has never appeared on a call and who paused the 218k solution on a 'broader SaaS and internal build strategy' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-03-27); he remains unengaged with Zycus and is unnamed in Salesforce, so he cannot be promoted to a named stakeholder. For the design engagement the business sponsor is still unnamed: on 2026-06-12 Lynn Dudley-Owen said 'we would need a sponsor... it's gotta be the bigwigs' and floated Grant Hardy (top, not attending) and 'Amelia/Emilia' as transcript-only possibilities, none confirmed in Salesforce. Adrian Jacobs (Decision Maker, Contract and Commercial Management Specialist) controls the contract but not the budget decision. The move is to get Lynn to formally name the design-engagement sponsor and to build a buy-vs-build case aimed at the CIO.

decision_criteria.narrative: For the live design engagement the criteria are explicit: a technology-agnostic, system-agnostic view of the to-be S2C processes and operating model, with regulatory third-party-risk controls embedded (Isabella Rankine, Legal Manager, 2026-04-28: 'we have a lot of responsibilities... from a regulatory point of view for third party risk management... a lot of these risk controls can be embedded in the process'), coverage of the payment/invoicing tail beyond source-to-contract (Johan Kotze, 2026-04-28), and local South African financial-services expertise paired with Zycus global-bank process depth (Kamen, Letsema, 2026-04-28). For the paused solution, the underlying criterion has shifted to buy-vs-build: Capitec must be convinced a bought platform beats internal build. Criteria are confirmed for the design phase and inferred for the eventual solution decision.

decision_process.narrative: The process is well mapped from the LOI onward. The 2025-12-12 LOI laid out Capitec's approval flow: internal contract review, supplier + Capitec contract review (multiple rounds), IT review (done in parallel), then Delegation of Authority approval, then signature. Reading the call sequence, the March 2026 escalation to an internal AI/CTO vetting board and the CIO's SaaS/internal-build strategy overrode that flow and paused the solution (Next_Step__c 2026-03-02, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-27). The deal then re-formed as a design engagement whose own process is a 10-week Letsema/Zycus plan (kickoff July 7-8, eight workshops July 21-Aug 11, blueprint and validation by ~week 10; Jitesh, 2026-06-12), governed by fortnightly steer-co check-ins with the relevant stakeholder on the design SOW/MSA is the current gate and it is stalled on Adrian.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are advanced but bottlenecked. As of 2026-06-12 the MSA has completed two rounds of redlining, the SOW plus a detailed scope document were sent and sit with Adrian Jacobs with no redlines returned, and an Order Form is in play; Zycus legal (Zoheb Amin, with Hrishikesh coordinating) had the SOW under final review, expected done by early June (Hrishikesh, 2026-06-05: 'our legal team is already on the contract to review... completed today or by max Monday'). Signature was targeted for late June, with Zycus wanting delivery staff onboarded by July 13 citing 7-10 day India visa and yellow-fever-vaccination lead times (Hrishikesh, 2026-06-05); it is now overdue. As a bank, Capitec requires regulatory third-party-risk and InfoSec review (Isabella Rankine, 2026-04-28; Fahiem Jattiem, Information Security Specialist, on the roster). The visibility gap is that Zycus does not know who at Capitec is actually reviewing the SOW.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed qualitatively: fragmented and under-documented procurement processes in constant flux ('the team's in a constant change management cycle, and that documentation is probably outdated'; end-to-end map 'outdated already', Kamen, 2026-04-28), regulatory third-party-risk controls that must be embedded in contracting given Capitec is a bank (Isabella Rankine, 2026-04-28), and a missing payment/invoicing tail beyond source-to-contract (Johan Kotze, 2026-04-28). The pain owner is the procurement function led by Carmen Marais (Procurement Manager), described as the workshop lead on the 2026-06-05 call. The pain is real and articulated but never quantified, which is precisely why the buyer chose a design-first path and why a numbers-based case is still missing.

champion.narrative: Lynn Dudley-Owen is a developing champion for the design engagement: she drives the schedule, coordinates stakeholders, pushes the kickoff into diaries, arranges Teams access, and set a weekly touch-base cadence ('we can touch base once a week', 2026-06-12). What has developed is strong operational momentum and access to Capitec's project machinery; what is still missing is executive reach, because she is delivery-side and said plainly the sponsor 'wouldn't be any of us... it's gotta be the bigwigs.' Adrian Jacobs (Decision Maker) is a transparent coach who executes the CIO's pause and now holds the contract, not an advocate pushing Zycus over an internal build. No champion yet carries the buy-vs-build case to the CIO, which is the single biggest gap.

competition.narrative: The live competitive dynamic is Zycus versus a Capitec internal-build strategy sponsored by the CIO, which paused the solution deal in March 2026 (SF Next_Step__c 2026-03-27). There is no named external SaaS rival (Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Ivalua) in any call, field, or task, so this is a build-vs-buy fight, not a vendor bake-off. Letsema (heard on calls as 'Letsima'/'CEMA', led by Kamen and Jitesh) is Zycus's co-delivery partner on the design engagement, not a competitor. Zycus wins by using the technology-agnostic design engagement to prove domain depth and platform maturity, embedding AI/Merlin value into the to-be blueprint, and quantifying the internal-build TCO and time-to-production disadvantage before the post-September solution decision.

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is live but rep-led: Lynn Dudley-Owen actively drives design-engagement scheduling on weekly Avoma touch-bases (2026-06-01, 06-05, 06-12), while the contract signature is stalled on Adrian Jacobs, who is sitting on the SOW/MSA past the late-June target. The 218k solution has been paused since March behind Capitec's CIO-sponsored internal-build strategy.

items.change: Kickoff locked for July 7 (remote) with eight workshops scheduled July 21-Aug 11 and a fortnightly steer-co cadence agreed with Lynn.
SOW and detailed scope document sent to Adrian Jacobs; MSA at two rounds of redlining; no SOW redlines returned.
Zycus legal (Zoheb Amin) completed SOW review and sent it across; Zycus 'on track from the contract's perspective'.
CloseDate pushed 2026-09-30 -> 2026-12-31 by owner, reflecting the paused solution scope.
Deal pivoted from a full 218k S2C solution to a technology-agnostic process-design engagement; solution put out of scope by Capitec's CIO/internal-build strategy.

competitive_position.summary: This is a build-vs-buy contest, not a vendor bake-off. The single strongest current threat is Capitec's internal-build strategy, sponsored by a newly influential CIO who paused the 218k solution in March 2026 on a broader SaaS/internal-build direction and remains unengaged with Zycus. No external SaaS rival is named in any source. Letsema is Zycus's co-delivery partner, not a competitor. Zycus's live wedge is the technology-agnostic design engagement, which keeps it inside the account and builds the platform-value and TCO proof needed to re-open the solution conversation post-September.

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify internal-build TCO and time-to-production disadvantage during the design engagement and hand the CIO a buy-vs-build case anchored to Capitec's own baseline.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Capitec expects a technology-agnostic, regulator-aware process-design engagement covering the full procurement landscape (not just S2C), delivered jointly by Zycus and Letsema; Zycus is well aligned on delivery but exposed on the commercial/solution decision, which the buyer has deferred.

De-risk the stalled SOW by opening a parallel legal contact (Isabella Rankine) instead of single-threading signature through Adrian Jacobs.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is present only as one of several design principles Capitec asked to see in the proposal; the buyer explicitly de-prioritised tool and AI selection in favour of technology-agnostic process redesign, so Merlin/agentic value is currently under-positioned and parked for a post-design solution decision.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 Moderate matches the calls: Capitec asked for AI/bot design principles in the proposal but put the solution (including Merlin) out of scope for now, choosing process redesign first. AI is a future consideration, not the buying driver.

items.detail: Scope reduced from a 218k S2C SaaS solution to a small technology-agnostic design engagement; the buyer is getting defensive and phasing spend, deferring the platform buy.
The budget owner (Capitec CIO) sponsors an internal-build strategy, has never been on a call, and paused the solution; no Zycus champion reaches him.
Design-engagement signature is overdue with the SOW/MSA sitting un-returned with Adrian Jacobs and no visibility into who is reviewing it.
The July 7 kickoff and July 21-Aug 11 workshops are being scheduled ahead of an unsigned contract, risking a slip if signature is not secured this week.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live but rep-led on the design engagement (weekly Lynn touch-bases through 2026-06-12); the deciding thread, the solution buy-vs-build decision, is cold and controlled by an unengaged CIO.
items: Weekly two-way cadence with Lynn Dudley-Owen sustained June 1, 5, 12.
Contract signature overdue and stalled on Adrian since mid-June.
Solution decision paused since March and parked to post-September.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (fragmented, under-documented, constantly-changing procurement processes; ~18 first-line staff); no cost, cycle-time, or savings figures quantified.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — In March 2026 Capitec's CIO paused the solution on a broader SaaS/internal-build strategy and the deal re-formed as a smaller design-first engagement; the buyer (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Next Step carries 19 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 35 is carried by momentum 70 vs stage-expected 52; competitive strength +0.50 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — In March 2026 Capitec's CIO paused the solution on a broader SaaS/internal-build strategy and the deal re-formed….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 70 (moving forward): Next Step carries 19 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 22 is anchored on the stage win (35), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 70, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Capitec Bank — Formal Evaluation · $218K · closes 31 Dec
cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

Signature was targeted for late June, with Zycus wanting delivery staff onboarded by July 13 citing 7-10 day India visa and yellow-fever-vaccination lead times (Hrishikesh, 2026-06-05); it is now overdue. As a bank, Capitec requires regulatory third-party-risk and InfoSec review (Isabella Rankine, 2026-04-28; Fahiem Jattiem, Information Security Specialist, on the roster). The visibility gap is that Zycus does not know who at Capitec is actually reviewing the SOW.

bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Lynn Dudley-Owen is a developing champion for the design engagement, driving scheduling, stakeholder coordination, and a weekly cadence, but she is…
bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Lynn Dudley-Owen is a developing champion for the design engagement, driving scheduling, stakeholder coordination, and a weekly cadence, but she is delivery-side with no budget or executive authority and no line to the CIO. Adrian Jacobs is a coach, not an advocate. No champion currently carries the buy-vs-build case to power.

bullets.text: ROI / business case landed — No quantified business case exists on any call or Salesforce field.
bullets.full: ROI / business case landed — No quantified business case exists on any call or Salesforce field. Capitec articulated qualitative pain only: on the 2026-04-28 scoping call Kamen (Letsema) sized procurement at roughly 18 first-line staff plus about 30 more being centralized on PO creation, and said processes had 'gone through the most changes we have in the last 5 years' with 'documentation probably outdated' and the end-to-end map 'outdated already.' No cycle-time, cost-per-transaction, FTE-hour, error-rate, or savings figure has ever been stated. The 10-week design engagement is itself scoped to surface current-state metrics, so the move is to use its discovery workshops to quantify baseline pain and build an ROI/TCO case against the internal-build alternative.

bullets.text: Scope reduced from a 218k S2C SaaS solution to a small technology-agnostic design engagement; the buyer is getting defensive and phasing spend, deferring the…
Live next steps with named dates

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.

bullets.text: Scope reduced from a 218k S2C SaaS solution to a small technology-agnostic design engagement; the buyer is getting defensive and phasing spend, deferring the platform…
The budget owner (Capitec CIO) sponsors an internal-build strategy, has never been on a call, and paused the solution; no Zycus champion reaches him.
Design-engagement signature is overdue with the SOW/MSA sitting un-returned with Adrian Jacobs and no visibility into who is reviewing it.
The July 7 kickoff and July 21-Aug 11 workshops are being scheduled ahead of an unsigned contract, risking a slip if signature is not secured this week.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: This is a build-vs-buy contest, not a vendor bake-off.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Confirm the SOW reviewer and secure Adrian's redlines before the July 7 kickoff. (by 8 Jul)
Get Lynn to name the design-engagement business sponsor and bring them into the kickoff. (by 15 Jul)
Use the design workshops to quantify Capitec's baseline and build a buy-vs-build TCO case for the CIO. (by 5 Aug)

gaps: Economic buyer (Capitec CIO) unnamed in Salesforce and never on a call; cannot be promoted to a named stakeholder.
Design-engagement business sponsor named only in transcript ('Amelia/Emilia', Grant Hardy), unconfirmed in Salesforce.
No quantified business case or baseline metrics for the procurement pain.
